name: WR001
desc:
Juvenile Ranch facility, faucet behind sign.
---
WATER ALERT: Next northbound water after the Juvenile Ranch may be the Lake Morena Campground in 18.7 miles. Check the pctwater.com for current water information.

name: Campo
desc: Campo Green Store, small convenience store. Common PCT start town.

name: WRCS015
desc: Hauser Creek, often dry, several campsites W of trail. Nearby unpaved Hauser Creek road is blocked to vehicle traffic.

name: LakeMorenaCG
desc:
Trailhead parking near Lake Morena Campground. PCT joins paved Lake Shore Drive for 2/10 mile N. Campground office is 3/10 mile NW on Lake Morena Drive. Lake Morena Village with small store/deli nearby.
---
WATER ALERT: Next southbound water after Lake Morena Campground may be the faucet at the Juvenile Ranch facility in 18.7 miles. Check pctwater.com for the latest water information.

name: Lake_Morena
desc: Lake Morena County Park, store, campground. Common first resupply.

name: BoulderOakCG
desc: Boulder Oaks Campground, outhouse, water, picnic tables.

name: WRCS030
desc: Seasonal Yellow Rose Spring, 4/10 mile N on Kitchen Creek Rd, often has water early in the hiking season, good camping if you don't mind the nearby (but seldom used) road.

name: WRCS032
desc: Fred Canyon, usually dry, campsite W of trail.

name: RD0033
desc: Unpaved Fred Canyon Rd, access to Cibbets Flat Campground.

name: CibbetsCG
desc: Cibbets Flat Campground, 8/10 mile NW of PCT, water, outhouse, picnic tables.

name: WRCS039
desc: Lower Morris Meadow, spring, campsite, 3/10 mile NW of PCT.

name: TR0041
desc: Trail junction to Burnt Ranchera Campground and the community of Mount Laguna.

name: BurntRanchCG
desc: Burnt Rancheria Campground, water, showers, [usually open mid-April to October].

name: Mount_Laguna
desc: Laguna Mountain Lodge, store. Accepts resupply packages.

name: LagunaCG
desc: Mount Laguna Campground, 7/10 mile SW of PCT, fee, coin-operated showers, water, open year round.

name: Deck0048
desc: Observation deck, trailside bench, access to Mount Laguna Campground 7/10 mile SW. Follow paved Sunrise Highway 2/10 mile S to the campground entrance road.

name: WR053
desc:
Pioneer Mail Picnic Area, outhouse.
---
"Remote Camping" not allowed in the Laguna Mountain Recreation Area.

name: WRCS059
desc: Sunrise Trailhead, outhouse, camping in the open field, water 5/10 mile W of PCT mile 59.5. Can be very windy camping at this location.

name: CS0064
desc: Campsite near dry creekbed just S of Chariot Cyn Rd.

name: WRCS068
desc:
Rodriguez Spur fire tank, campsite.
---
WATER ALERT: Next northbound water after the Rodriguez Spur fire tank may be Barrel Spring in 32.7 miles. This water source was once reliable, but has gone dry sometimes in 2016 & 2017, check the pctwater.com for current water information.

name: Julian
desc: Julian, ~12 miles off trail. Full resupply, famous apple pie. Hitch required.

name: CS0079
desc: Campsite in sandy dry creek bed

name: CS0080C
desc: Small sandy campsite under dry waterfall, another campsite in the wash below.

name: CS0082
desc: Several small campsites in wash below the trail.

name: CS0082B
desc: Small campsite next to trail, view.

name: BV0085
desc: Small bivy campsite in a gully.

name: CS0085C
desc: Large campsite, better than campsites in the burn area 200 feet before.

name: CS0086
desc: Several campsites on saddle of ridge.

name: CS0087
desc: Many campsites along wide dry creekbed.

name: CS091
desc: Several small campsites 100 ft N of the 3rd gate, possible shade from bushes, junction with trail to water cache and underground water cistern E of the PCT.

name: CS0094
desc: Several small campsites on small ridge, may be hidden from trail by brush.

name: WRCS101
desc:
Barrel Spring, trough, shade, and campsites. Watch for Poison Oak.
---
WATER ALERT: Next southbound water after Barrel Spring may be the Rodriguez fire tank in 32.7 miles. Check the pctwater.com for current information.

name: Warner_Springs
desc: Warner Springs Community Resource Center. Limited resupply, accepts packages.

name: CS0115
desc: Campsite on E side of Agua Caliente Creek

name: CS0124
desc: Several small campsites among the boulders.

name: CS0129
desc: Several campsites on the high ridge near Combs Peak.

name: WRCS140B
desc: Nance Canyon, seasonal creek, several sandy beach-like campsites downstream from trail crossing.

name: WRCS169
desc: Apache Spring, 1/2 mile E of PCT, several large campsites, can be very windy.

name: Idyllwild
desc: Idyllwild, ~5 miles off trail. Full hiker services, excellent resupply.

name: SanJacintoWild2
desc: San Jacinto State Park Wilderness, northern boundary. Camping allowed only in designated camping areas, $5 per person permit required, for 5.4 miles to the S.

name: BlackMtnGC-BMR
desc: Black Mountain group camping area - mi 14

name: WRCS194
desc: Seasonal headwaters of the West Fork Snow Creek, campsite, 1/10 mile S of PCT on unmarked use trail.

name: CS0202
desc: Campsite at the saddle W of knob.

name: WR206
desc:
Snow Canyon Road faucet, campsites 150 feet W, PCT joins paved Snow Creek Road.
---
WATER ALERT: Next southbound water after Snow Canyon Road faucet may be N Fork San Jacinto River Tributary in 19.5 miles.

name: WRCS0219
desc: Whitewater Preserve [wildlandsconservancy.org, 760-325-7222], 6/10 mile E of PCT, shade, water, wading pool, toilets, and free camping for PCT hikers.

name: WRCS0239
desc: Forested Flats Junction, seasonal water, campsites nearby.

name: WRCS0240B
desc:
Mission Creek Trail Camp spring, parking, campsites.
---
WATER ALERT: Next northbound water after Mission Creek Trail Camp spring may be Arrastre Trail Camp in 16.3 miles.

name: WRCS0240
desc: Mission Creek Trail Camp spring, parking, campsites, PCT joins an unpaved road for a short distance.

name: WR0240
desc: Spring and trough near Mission Creek Trail Camp, other spring is usually better.

name: RD0246
desc: Unpaved road near the Coon Creek Group Campground.

name: CS0246
desc: Coon Creek Cabin Group Campground, no water.

name: WRCS0256
desc:
Arrastre Trail Camp, Deer Spring, stream 200 feet to the N, picnic table, campsites.
---
WATER ALERT: Next southbound water after Arrastre Trail Camp, Deer Spring, may be Mission Springs Trail Camp in 16.3 miles.

name: Big_Bear_Lake
desc: Big Bear Lake, ~2 miles off trail. Full hiker services, large grocery store.

name: WR0269
desc: Doble Trail Camp, outhouse, picnic table, water, corral.

name: TR0269
desc: Trail junction to Doble Trail Camp.

name: WRCS0275
desc: Caribou Creek, small wooden bridge, campsite nearby.

name: CS0286
desc: Little Bear Spring trail camp, picnic table, outhouse, corral.

name: CS0293
desc: Large campsite, oak trees, small seasonal stream nearby.

name: SplintersCabin
desc: Splinters Cabin, outhouse, 1/10 mile SW of PCT. Water nearby.

name: DeepCreekHotSpring
desc: Deep Creek hot spring. Popular dispersed camping area on BLM land.

name: TR0318
desc: Mojave River Campground trail junction. Campground is 3/4 mile off trail.

name: WRCS0318
desc: Seasonal Grass Valley Creek, campsite nearby.

name: CrowderCanyonCamp
desc: Crowder Canyon, small seasonal creek. Dispersed BLM camping area near creek.

name: CajonPass_McDonalds
desc: McDonald's Restaurant and Best Western, 3/10 mile NW of PCT. Water, food, cell service. Common PCT stop.

name: CS0362
desc: Campsite near the end of an unpaved road.

name: WR0364
desc:
Guffy Spring, 1/10 mile N of the PCT. Follow use trail about 1/10 mile before campground]
---
WATER ALERT: Next water southbound after Guffy Spring may be Cajon Pass in 22.3 miles.

name: Wrightwood
desc: Wrightwood, ~5 miles off trail. Full hiker services.

name: CS0384
desc: Little Jimmy Campground. The PCT goes right at the unsigned trail just before the campground.

name: EaglesRoost
desc:
Eagles Roost Picnic Area, outhouse, picnic tables, trash cans, paved parking area. Endangered species bypass.
---
The once thriving Mountain Yellow-Legged Frog is now nearly extinct. To protect the frog, the PCT is closed for 2.7 miles from Eagles Roost to the Burkhart Trail junction. Hikers can bypass the closure by road walking 2.7 miles along Hwy 2 to Buckhorn Campground, then follow the campground road and Burkhart Trail 2.2 miles to rejoin the PCT.

name: WRCS0395
desc: Cooper Canyon Trail Camp, seasonal creek nearby.

name: ActonKOA
desc: Acton KOA RV park [koa.com/campgrounds/acton/, 661-268-1214], 2/10 mile E of PCT with camping, showers, laundry, and a small store. To reach the KOA cross Soledad Canyon Rd and walk N on the PCT down the embankment. Look to the right and you will see the KOA campground across the flat open area. If you cross the creek you have gone too far.

name: Agua_Dulce
desc: Agua Dulce, on trail. Vasquez Rocks area. Hiker-friendly town.

name: WRCS494
desc: Upper Shake Campground, 2/10 mile N of PCT. Spring is on the floor of Shake Canyon 100 yards N of the campground. From the first campsite E of the campground entrance sign, walk N down the steep hill into Shake Canyon N of PCT.

name: SawmillCG
desc: Sawmill Campground, 3/10 mile S of PCT.

name: WR508
desc: Spring in canyon 2/10 mile W of Horse Camp.

name: CS0508
desc: Horse Camp, picnic table, trail to spring in canyon below.

name: Hikertown
desc: Hikertown, water spigots, accepts hiker packages, camping permitted. Key stop before Mojave aqueduct walk.

name: CottonwoodCreekBridge
desc: Faucet near Cottonwood Creek bridge. Water when aqueduct is flowing. Common stealth camp area.

name: WRCS542
desc:
Tylerhorse Canyon, small creek, campsite.
---
WATER ALERT: Next northbound water after Tylerhorse Canyon may be Oak Creek in 16.6 miles.

name: OakCreekCamp
desc: Oak Creek, dispersed BLM camping near creek crossing. Last reliable water before Tehachapi resupply.

name: Tehachapi
desc: Tehachapi, ~16 miles off trail via hitch. Full resupply services.

name: Mojave
desc: Mojave, ~14 miles off trail. Full services including Walmart.

name: WRCS602
desc:
Robin Bird Spring, 1/10 mile W of PCT, campsite.
---
WATER ALERT: Next southbound water after Robin Bird Spring may be Golden Oak Spring in 18.8 miles.

name: LandersCamp
desc: Landers Camp, spring, fire tank, 2/10 mile N of PCT. Named camp, reliable spring.

name: KelsoValleyRoad
desc: Kelso Valley Road crossing. Open BLM dispersed camping at road junction, water nearby.

name: BirdSpringPass
desc: Bird Spring Pass, unpaved Road SC120. Open BLM dispersed camping area.

name: CS0651
desc:
Walker Pass Campground, with picnic tables, and outhouse. This campground usually does not have water.
---
Spring-fed cisterns 2/10 mile NW of the campground may have water. Follow Hwy 178 for 250 yards W from the campground entrance and look to the left. Check pctwater.com.

name: Walker_Pass
desc: Walker Pass Trailhead. Limited resupply cache. Last stop before Kennedy Meadows.

name: CS0676
desc: Several small campsites along a ridge.

name: ChimneyCreekCG
desc: Chimney Creek Campground, 2/10 mile E of PCT via Canebreak Road. Outhouse, seasonal water.

name: RD0681
desc: Unpaved Canebreak Road to Chimney Creek campground 2/10 mile to the E.

name: FoxMillSpring
desc: Fox Mill Spring and trough. BLM open camping near water source.

name: ManterCreek
desc: Manter Creek, first creek in Rockhouse Basin. Open dispersed camping.

name: SForkKernRiver
desc: South Fork Kern River. Open BLM dispersed camping near river.

name: Kennedy_Meadows
desc: Kennedy Meadows General Store and Grumpy Bear's Retreat. Rite of passage resupply, marks end of desert and start of High Sierra.

name: RD0704
desc: Paved Kennedy Meadows Road to campground.

name: KennedyMeadowsCG
desc: Kennedy Meadows Campground, paved parking, outhouse, water from stream. Adjacent to Kennedy Meadows Store.

name: KennedyMdwCG
desc: Kennedy Meadows Campground, paved parking area, outhouse, water from spigot or nearby creek, trail register on a post at the road.

name: CS0713
desc: Campsite in the trees 500 feet E of PCT near small seasonal creek.

name: WACS0716
desc: South Fork Kern River, steel bridge, campsite.

name: WACS0731
desc: Death Canyon Creek, large camping area.

name: TR0742
desc: Trail junction to Diaz Creek, large campsites nearby.

name: WACS0742
desc: Diaz Creek, campsite, 1/10 mile E of PCT.

name: TR0745
desc: Mulkey Pass and Horseshoe Meadow Trail junctions. Horseshoe Meadow, 1.7 miles N of the PCT, campground, water, toilet, trash, paved road.

name: WACSBB0760
desc: Rock Creek, several campsites, bear box.

name: WACSBB0766
desc: Several campsites, bear locker, creek nearby.

name: WACSBB0767B
desc: Campsite, water, bear box, Crabtree Ranger, 1 mile NE of PCT.

name: CS0767
desc: Several large campsites, 7/10 mile E of PCT.

name: WACSBB0770B
desc: Wallace Creek ford, water, campsites, bear locker S of creek.

name: WACSBB0774
desc: Water, several campsites, bear box, near the Tyndall Frog Ponds.

name: WACSBB0775B
desc: Campsite, water, bear locker, Tyndall Creek nearby.

name: WACS0779
desc: Several small exposed campsites below Forester Pass, water nearby.

name: WACS0781
desc: Water, several small exposed campsites at the lake outlet.

name: WACS0783
desc: Campsite, water from creek south of trail.

name: WACSBB0784B
desc: Campsite, bear box, water from nearby Bubbs Creek.

name: WACS0785
desc: Campsite, water from nearby Bubbs Creek.

name: WACSBB0786
desc: Upper Vidette Meadow, large campsite, water, bear box.

name: WACSBB0787
desc: Middle Vidette Meadow, large campsites, bear locker, water from Bubbs Creek, more campsites 1/10 mi N or S of the bear locker.

name: Independence
desc: Independence, ~17 miles off trail via Kearsarge Pass. Full hiker services.

name: TR0793
desc: Trail junction to bear locker and campsite.

name: WACSBB0793B
desc: Middle Rae Lake, campsite, bear locker.

name: WACSBB0794B
desc: Lower Rae Lake, campsite, bear locker.

name: WACSBB0795
desc: Arrowhead Lake, large campsite, bear locker.

name: WACSBB0800
desc: Woods Creek, suspension bridge, campsites, bear locker.

desc: South Fork of the Kings River ford [a large river, sometimes difficult], area closed to camping.

name: WACS0812
desc: Campsite, water, along South Fork of Kings River.

name: WACS0820
desc: Small stream, small campsites 100 feet S of the stream crossing.

name: WACS0824B
desc: Large campsite, 200 feet S of trail, toward Palisade Creek.

name: WACS0826
desc: Campsite near Palisade Creek, also several other campsites 1/10 mile E.

name: WACS0827
desc: Campsite, Middle Fork Kings River nearby.

name: WACS0828
desc: Campsite, Middle Fork Kings River nearby.

desc: Campsite along the Middle Fork Kings River.

name: WACS0831
desc: Campsite, water, along Middle Fork Kings River.

name: WACS0831B
desc: Campsite, water, along Middle Fork Kings River.

name: WACS0831C
desc: Campsite, water, along Middle Fork Kings River.

name: WACS0833C
desc: Large campsites, Middle Fork Kings River nearby.

name: WACS0833D
desc: Large campsite along Middle Fork Kings River.

name: WACS0834
desc: Large campsite along the Middle Fork Kings River.

name: WACS0835
desc: Campsite along the Middle Fork Kings River.

name: WACS0835C
desc: Campsite along the Middle Fork Kings River.

name: WA0836
desc: Middle Fork Kings River, inlet to small lake, camping not permitted in this area.

name: WACS0851
desc: Evolution Creek ford, campsites, can be a dangerous crossing early season during high water.

name: WACS0852B
desc: South Fork San Joaquin River, wood bridge, several campsites, water, Goddard Canyon Trail junction.

name: WACS0853E
desc: South Fork San Joaquin River, steel bridge, large campsite nearby.

name: WACS0855
desc: Campsite in Aspen Meadow, water, another campsite 1/10th mile S.

name: WACS0856B
desc: Large campsite along the South Fork San Joaquin River.

name: WACS0858
desc: Campsite along the South Fork San Joaquin River, 4/10 mile NW of PCT.

name: WACS0858B
desc: Campsite near Muir Ranch, 1.6 miles NW of PCT on Florence Lake Trail.

name: Muir_Trail_Ranch
desc: Muir Trail Ranch, 1.5 miles NW of PCT via Florence Lake Trail. Accepts resupply buckets (must pre-arrange). Hot spring nearby. Popular JMT/PCT stop.

name: CS0862B
desc: Several campsites near a seasonal creek

name: Vermilion_Valley_Resort
desc: Vermilion Valley Resort, 6 miles via Lake Edison ferry or Bear Ridge Trail from VVR Ferry junction. Store, restaurant, cabins. Accepts packages ($40 fee). Key Sierra resupply.

name: WACS0864
desc: Sallie Keyes Lake outlet, campsites nearby.

name: WACS0868B
desc: West Fork Bear Creek, campsite S of the creek.

name: WACSBB0879C
desc: Campsite, bear locker, 1.5 miles W of PCT.

name: WACS0879
desc: Mono Creek, steel bridge, several campsites on either side of the bridge.

name: WACS0879B
desc: North Fork Mono Creek ford, campsites at the ford and 400 feet E.

name: MonoPassTR
desc: Mono Pass Trail junction, campsites nearby.

name: WACS0881
desc: North Fork Mono Creek, near Pocket Meadow, campsite.

name: WACS0882
desc: North Fork Mono Creek, second ford, campsites nearby.

name: SilverPass2
desc: High point above Silver Pass, the pass is actually 2/10 mi S, campsite nearby.

name: WACS0896
desc: Stream, double log crossing, campsite nearby.

name: WACS0901
desc: Deer Creek, double log crossing, large campsite.

name: Mammoth_Lakes
desc: Mammoth Lakes, accessible via Red's Meadow shuttle. Full hiker services, excellent resupply.

name: RedsMdwCG
desc: Red's Meadow Campground, water, hot spring bath house, 6/10 mi N of PCT.

name: RedsMdwStore
desc: Red's Meadow [Redsmeadow.com, 760-934-2345] is a pack station 3/10 mile NE of the PCT with a small store, restaurant, cabins, laundry, pay phone, nearby campground with free hot spring showers. A shuttle to the town of Mammoth Lakes stops at the store. Red's Meadow is open from about 6/15 - 10/1. Snow may delay the opening.

name: TR0907
desc: Trail junction to Red's Meadow Store and campground, 3/10 mile NE of PCT.

name: RedsMdwTR3
desc: One of several trails to Reds Meadow Store, Campground, 6/10 mile N of PCT.

name: WACS0909T
desc: Ruby Lake, campsite, 13 miles N on JMT alternate.

name: WACS0909U
desc: Ruby Lake, campsite, 13.3 miles N on JMT alternate.

name: WACS0927
desc: Rush Creek ford, small campsite nearby.

name: Tuolumne_Meadows
desc: Tuolumne Meadows store and post office, Yosemite NP. Common end of SoCal section.
